title: Choosing Your Path: University or Vocational School
passage:
Choosing Your Path: University or Vocational School

Choosing what to do after high school is a big decision for young people. Some students choose to go to university. This is called higher education. Other students choose to go to a vocational school. This is called vocational training. Both choices are very useful, but they are different in many ways.

University programs usually last for three or four years. At university, students study general and theoretical subjects like history, science, or literature. Students spend a lot of time in classrooms. They read many books, write long essays, and listen to lectures from professors. University helps students get professional jobs such as teachers, doctors, lawyers, or scientists. However, university can be very expensive, and students often need to take loans to pay for their classes.

Vocational training is different because it focuses on practical skills. These programs are much shorter, usually lasting from six months to two years. Students do not spend much time reading books. Instead, they practice doing a job with their hands. For example, they learn how to fix cars, repair electricity, build houses, or cook in a professional kitchen. Vocational schools prepare students to become mechanics, electricians, builders, or chefs. These programs are usually much cheaper than university courses.

So, which option is better? It depends on what the student likes. If a student enjoys reading, researching, and studying theory, then university is a good choice. But if a student wants to start working quickly and likes doing physical work, vocational training is a great option. Both paths can help people find good jobs and build a successful future.

In many countries, governments want more young people to choose vocational training because businesses need more workers with practical skills. At the same time, universities are still popular because they offer a fun campus life and social activities. In the end, both choices are excellent for different kinds of people.

vocabulary.meaning: education at a college or university level
training that teaches you the skills you need to do a specific job
based on ideas and principles, not practical actions
money that is borrowed and must be paid back
skills learned by doing physical or hands-on activities

tactics:
Hướng dẫn làm bài (A1-A2)

1. Tìm từ khóa chính xác: Ở trình độ này, các câu hỏi sử dụng từ ngữ giống hệt trong bài đọc. Hãy quét (scan) các từ nổi bật như 'loans', 'scientists', 'hands' để tìm đúng đoạn văn chứa đáp án.

2. True/False/Not Given: Nếu câu hỏi có thông tin trái ngược hoàn toàn với bài đọc (ví dụ: đắt hơn vs rẻ hơn), chọn FALSE. Nếu thông tin không được nhắc tới trong bài (ví dụ: thời gian tìm được việc làm), chọn NOT GIVEN.

3. Điền từ: Chú ý giới hạn từ (NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS). Chỉ lấy từ có sẵn trong bài đọc, không tự ý thay đổi dạng của từ.
